description: 100-test retrieval benchmark for the three fixed 50/50 two-column PDFs. Single-hop and multi-hop tests are intentionally heavier, requiring multiple terms per evidence unit.

scoringNotes.expectedChunkContains: Each inner array is one evidence unit. A retrieved chunk is relevant if it contains all strings in an evidence unit and matches expectedSource when provided.
scoringNotes.single_hop: Heavy single-hop tests require one chunk to contain multiple related terms from one local finding.
scoringNotes.multi_hop: Pass StrictMultiHopRecall only if all evidence units appear in top K.
scoringNotes.precision: Relevant retrieved chunks divided by K, or evidence-unit precision if you prefer overlap-resistant scoring.
scoringNotes.mrr: Reciprocal rank of the first relevant retrieved chunk.
scoringNotes.boundary: Passes when boundaryTermsMustBeTogether appear together in a retrieved chunk.
scoringNotes.no_answer: False positive if retriever returns confident evidence for a fact that is not present.
